title: Euodia ternata
intro: Euodia ternata, also known as Chinese rowan or three-leafed Euodia, is a small tree or shrub native to China and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Euodia ternata include coumarins, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Euodia ternata is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, often in combination with other herbs, to take advantage of its health benefits.
